payload.oneLiner: Talar neck fractures are hindfoot injuries where displacement predicts avascular necrosis risk.

orSurvivalTips: Mention urgent reduction/fixation for displaced neck fractures

metadata.description: Proximal tibial articular surface bearing medial and lateral condylar load.
Hindfoot bone articulating with talus at the subtalar joint.

metadata.claim_text: Talus fractures carry avascular necrosis risk because talar neck displacement compromises limited retrograde blood supply.
Hawkins classification links talar neck displacement to subtalar and ankle subluxation and rising AVN risk.
Document mechanism, Hawkins grade language, reduction quality, fixation plan, and AVN surveillance strategy.

metadata.action: Urgent anatomic reduction and talus ORIF with attending; plan serial imaging for AVN surveillance
metadata.trigger: Displaced talar neck fracture (Hawkins II-IV pattern) with subtalar or ankle subluxation

metadata.action: Establish AVN surveillance with serial clinical exam and advanced imaging per attending protocol
metadata.trigger: Talar neck fracture treated operatively or nonoperatively with Hawkins displacement history

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Lower Extremity Trauma Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite pelvis-to-foot model linking pelvic ring, knee, leg, and hindfoot structures for trauma neighborhoods.
Quadriceps-patella-tibial tubercle linkage enabling knee extension.
Primary knee stabilizer commonly injured with tibial plateau and knee trauma.
Central foot region including navicular, cuneiforms, and cuboid linking hindfoot to forefoot.
Lateral pelvic ilium region relevant to acetabular column and pelvic ring injuries.
Posterior knee stabilizer relevant in high-energy knee and plateau injuries.
Major knee-region artery at risk in tibial plateau, distal femur, and knee dislocation trauma.
Plantar fascia and soft-tissue envelope relevant to calcaneus fracture swelling and wound risk.
Lateral knee nerve at risk in tibial plateau and proximal fibular injuries.
Tibiotalar and subtalar articular bone with limited vascular supply.
Tarsometatarsal articulation complex; instability defines Lisfranc injury.
Osteonecrosis of the talus after vascular compromise from talar neck fracture.
Open reduction internal fixation of displaced talar neck or body fracture.
Posterior pelvic anchor articulating with ilia at the sacroiliac joints.
Hip socket formed by ilium, ischium, and pubis; injured in acetabular fractures.
Anterior midline pelvic articulation; disruption indicates pelvic ring instability.
Talar neck fracture classification by subtalar and ankle joint subluxation/dislocation.
Radiographic talar neck fracture pattern with Hawkins displacement implications.
Distal femoral metaphysis and condylar region forming the proximal knee.
Posterior pelvic articulation between sacrum and ilium; key pelvic ring stability structure.
Bony pelvic ring formed by ilium, ischium, pubis, and sacrum.

metadata.claim_text: Delayed reduction of displaced talar neck fractures increases AVN risk — treat as urgent anatomic restoration problem.
Clearing ankle trauma without scrutinizing talar neck lines on radiographs when mechanism suggests talar injury.

metadata.description: Talar neck or body fracture with limited vascular supply and AVN risk after displacement.

relationship_metadata.note: displacement and Hawkins grade drive fixation urgency

metadata.description: Talocalcaneal articulation governing hindfoot alignment after calcaneus and talus injuries.
Femoral shaft between proximal and distal metaphyseal regions.
Sesamoid bone within the extensor mechanism at the anterior knee.
